UG Programme in
Finance and Economics with CA/CFA Pathway

A 4-year Bachelor's where you learn finance through hands-on experience and industry projects.

Apply Now

Bachelor's Programme in
Finance & Economics

Format

Full Time

Opt-in Residential

Commencement Date

August '26

Round 3 Deadline: 13th May '26

Eligibility

Class XIIth

Current Students &
Pass-outs (2024 onwards)

Duration

4 Years

Including CA or CFA
Pathway

Round 3 Starting Soon Apply Now
  • Highlights
  • Curriculum & Faculty
  • Admissions & Fees
  • Career Prospects
  • Class Profile

Build Real-World
Finance Skills

Master finance with guidance from leading CAs, CFAs and experts at KPMG,
Goldman Sachs, JP Morgan, etc.

Integrated Certification Pathway:
Opt for CA or CFA Training

Choose between the Chartered Accountancy (CA) Path or the globally recognised Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA) Path.

1

2-Year Articleship with CA preparation

2

Prepare for CFA Levels I–III under expert guidance

3

Target global roles across New York, London, Singapore

Start Your Articleship
at the Big 4+

Kick off your finance career with real-world work experience in CA firms & top financial institutions.

1

Articleships at EY, KPMG, Deloitte, PwC, etc.

2

200+ companies recruit for internships

3

85%+ students intern in the first year

Download Internship Report

Applied Hands on Curriculum Taught by
JP Morgan & Goldman Sachs Leaders

Practical curriculum that allows you to play different finance roles such as Equity Research Analyst, Fund Manager, and Investment Banker, through simulations.

1

8+ Outclass Challenges across 4 years

2

Pitch M&A deals, audit financials, & structure IPOs

3

Manage portfolios, evaluate startups, & advise HNIs

Explore Curriculum

Invest From a
5 Cr Student Fund

Run a ₹5 Cr investment fund and invest across equities, startups, crypto, real estate, and commodities.

1

10+ investments made every year

2

Trade in stocks, crypto & even real estate

3

65%+ returns generated in 2024–25

Download Report

100+ Empaneled
Financial Experts

Learn from top CAs, CFAs, and finance leaders from global giants like
EY, PwC, KPMG, Goldman Sachs, JP Morgan, and more.

Founder

Designed For Future Traders, Investment Bankers,
Fund Managers, Venture Capitalists, & CFOs

Learn 8 Major Financial Instruments

Equity | Debt | Derivatives | Crypto | Forex | Commodities | Real Estate

Optimise Capital Structure and Tax Outflow

Debt | Equity | Capital Expenditure | GST | Indirect Taxes | Income Tax

Balance Risk & Return Using Derivatives

Futures | Options | Forwards | Swaps | Hedging Strategies

Build Financial Models for Equity Valuation

DCF | Dividend Discount Model | Precedent Transaction Analysis

Understand Risk & Compliance

VaR Models | Stress Testing | SEBI | IPA | MiFID II | SEC

Audit Independently & Uphold Ethics

Audit Standards | Ethics Compliance | Reporting Integrity | Transparency

Master Fixed Income Instruments

Bonds | Debentures | Preferred Stocks | Interest Rate | Investor Confidence

Learn 8 Major Financial Instruments

Equity | Debt | Derivatives | Crypto | Forex | Commodities | Real Estate

Optimise Capital Structure and Tax Outflow

Debt | Equity | Capital Expenditure | GST | Indirect Taxes | Income Tax

Balance Risk & Return Using Derivatives

Futures | Options | Forwards | Swaps | Hedging Strategies

Build Financial Models for Equity Valuation

DCF | Dividend Discount Model | Precedent Transaction Analysis

Understand Risk & Compliance

VaR Models | Stress Testing | SEBI | IPA | MiFID II | SEC

Audit Independently & Uphold Ethics

Audit Standards | Ethics Compliance | Reporting Integrity | Transparency

Master Fixed Income Instruments

Bonds | Debentures | Preferred Stocks | Interest Rate | Investor Confidence

Internships with
Top Finance Firms

Begin your finance career with real-world internships with top companies like PwC, KPMG, EY, Paytm, Yes Bank, etc.

Ritvika Khanna

GST Filings & Audit, KPMG

Class of 2027

“During my internship at KPMG, I identified a ₹30 Cr tax benefit in my first week, a pivotal moment that greatly accelerated my growth. With a supportive manager who challenged me, I got opportunities to represent KPMG at conferences, expanding my expertise, network, and confidence.”

1/10

Pulastya Jain

Portfolio Analysis, SEP

Class of 2027

“At Sweat Equity Partners, I led market research and team interviews for a portfolio company, built a detailed financial model, and managed interns to create a grant pitch deck. This experience sharpened my analytical thinking, leadership, and communication skills.”

2/10

Sneha Panthary

Treasury, Paytm

Class of 2027

“During my Treasury internship at Paytm, I worked on compliance, audits, and mutual fund management. I gained deep insights into regulatory frameworks, supported internal audits, and strengthened my understanding of risk management and corporate treasury operations.”

3/10

Jay Khemka

Investment Analysis, Nandan Growth Fund

Class of 2027

“As an Investment Analyst, I evaluated investment opportunities alongside bankers, analysing financials, assessing risks, and identifying gaps. I also participated in CEO meetings during deal negotiations, gaining exposure to strategic thinking and high-stakes financial decision-making.”

4/10

Shivangi Duttaupadhyay

Spectrum Banking, Yes Bank

Class of 2027

“At Yes Bank, I worked on wealth management and equity advisory, analysing equity options, tracking stock performance, and recommending investments. I engaged with 15–20 HNIs, gaining insights into client profiling, risk appetite, and personalised financial planning.”

5/10

Palak Dua

Risk & Audit, EY

“Working with the Risk and Audit team at a Big 4 firm as a first-year student gave me hands-on exposure to financial planning, quantitative analysis, and compliance. I built strong skills in financial modelling and gained a solid grasp of India’s regulatory framework.”

6/10

Ananya Singhania

Strategic Planning & Market Analysis, PwC

Class of 2027

“At PwC, I was entrusted with ownership from day one, enhancing internal financial analysis tools and collaborating with cross-functional teams. This experience provided me with valuable insights into global financial systems and market dynamics.”

7/10

Dhruv Lakra

Venture Capital, Sauce.VC

Class of 2027

“At Sauce VC, I sourced 15+ early-stage consumer startups, reviewed 20+ pitch decks, and conducted due diligence via founder calls, surveys, and market research. I also built category trackers to spot trends and learned how investors evaluate founders, scalability, and differentiation.”

8/10

Gunit Royzada

Investment Research, Analysis & Reporting, Waterfield

Class of 2027

“At Waterfield Advisors, India's largest multi-family office, I worked in the Global Investment Advisory team, analysing offshore mutual funds, reviewing UHNI portfolios, and automating workflows using Excel, Bloomberg, and Morningstar. This gave me direct exposure to global investment strategies and portfolio management.”

9/10

Devansh Asawa

Business Finance, Shadowfax

Class of 2027

“During my internship at Shadowfax, I handled financial reconciliations and dispute resolution for clients like Tata Cliq and Hopscotch. I also worked on cost optimisation by analysing expenses and negotiating vendor rates, which directly improved operational efficiency and boosted profit margins.”

10/10

Learn by Doing
Not Just by Sitting in Lectures

Students learn as much outside the classroom as they do inside it, with industry-relevant challenges that simulate real-world finance, accounting, and investment roles.

Study Abroad Programme

Study Abroad to
Gain Global Exposure

Study abroad for summer school or short duration immersion at prestigious global B-Schools like Babson College, IESEG France, SDA Bocconi, Harvard etc and gain unmatched insights, while experiencing life in a new country.

Beat The Sensex

Outperform the Market:
Trade with Real Capital

  • Backed by a ₹50L fund from Masters’ Union
  • Active investments in stocks, bonds, commodities, & crypto
  • 53% realised gains over 3 years, beating both Sensex & Nifty

10 Cr. AUM Challenge

Establish Your Own
Wealth Management Firm

  • Target to get ₹10 Cr. in AUM within 1 year
  • Learn how to sell to funds & LPs
  • Network with & raise capital from UHNIs

Investment Banking Challenge

Lead Real M&A Deals:
Evaluate and Pitch Strategies

  • Evaluate acquisition targets for clients like Reliance, Cipla
  • Estimate fair value, synergies, and potential deal structures
  • Conduct diligence and draft M&A strategy for management

PE/VC Challenge

Evaluate Startups:
Invest Like a Venture Capitalist

  • Develop sector thesis and analyse trends & competition
  • Spot investments aligned with thesis and problem-founder fit
  • Recommend investments and project potential exits

The Financial Audit Challenge

Own Financial Integrity &
Tax Compliance for a Firm

  • Reconcile statements to identify errors or mismatches
  • File accurate tax returns across income, TDS, and GST
  • Optimise taxes through compliant deductions and structuring

Masters’ Union Investment Fund Real Investment, Real Results

A student-managed fund that provides hands-on investment experience across diverse asset classes, including equities, crypto, real estate, and startups.

Watch Video

Masters’ Union Investment Fund
Real Investment, Real Results

A student-managed fund that provides hands-on investment
experience across diverse asset classes, including equities, crypto, real estate, and startups.

Learn with Our Expert Community

Become a part of our deeply engaged community consisting of seasoned & aspiring Traders, Investment Bankers, Fund Managers, Venture Capitalists, etc. to gain insights, share strategies, and grow your financial expertise.

1

Receive real-time insights & feedback from industry experts

2

Make faster, data-backed decisions with expert support

3

Learn from Upstox experts leveraging Bloomberg Terminal

4

Structured, expert-driven learning with 1:1 mentorship

5

300+ AMA sessions by leading finance experts

“What are derivatives?”

“How to select stocks?”

“How do I hedge risks ?”

Global Immersion Programme in
UK, US, Singapore, and Europe

Travel to global financial hubs and experience firsthand the dynamics of financial markets, investment banking, and wealth management in top cities like London, New York, and Singapore.

Download Singapore GIP Report

Student Stories

Glimpse of Student Life

Engage with a
Comprehensive Finance Curriculum

Explore modules that blend accounting, auditing, economics, and financial
management, preparing you for a successful career in global finance.

Integrated CA or CFA Training: Choose Your Pathway

Study at World Renowned B-Schools

Experience world-class business education from one
of Europe’s finest institutions.

Chartered Accountancy
Pathway

For students pursuing careers in accounting, taxation, and auditing.

  • 1

    CA Prep with 2-Year Articleship at Big 4+
    Train for CA Foundation, Inter, & Final with a 2-year articleship.

  • 2

    Specialise in Audit, Tax & Corporate Finance
    Work on client audits, tax filings, and financial analysis.

Internal Audit / Risk Analyst
Tax Analyst
Forensic Audit Analyst
Financial Controller Trainee
Compliance Officer Assistant

Chartered Financial Analyst
Pathway

For students aspiring for global careers in finance and investments.

  • 1

    CFA Prep with Industry Experience
    Train for CFA Levels I–III & work across IB, VC, & FinTech.

  • 2

    Specialise in Modelling & Financial Strategy
    Build DCF, LBO, & valuation models & master capital allocation & risk.

Investment Analyst (Buy-Side)
Equity Research Associate/Analyst
Valuation Analyst/Associate
Investment Banking Analyst
Credit Analyst

Enabling You to Excel
Inside and Outside the Classroom

  • CA PATHWAY
  • CFA PATHWAY

Lay the Foundations of Accounting and Business

Build a strong base in accounting, business law, economics, and statistics, developing the analytical mindset and professional discipline of a Chartered Accountant.

  • In Class : Core Courses
  • Hands-on Projects
  • Fundamentals of Accounting: How to read financials like an investor?
  • Microeconomics: How to predict demand and supply?
  • Macroeconomics: How a country’s economy work?
  • Business Statistics: How to decode the signal from the noise?
  • Business Law & Regulatory Framework: How to apply legal & regulatory framework?
  • Quantitative Techniques & Logical Reasoning: How to identify hidden risks?
  • Advanced Accounting; How to report, consolidate, and analyze a business?
  • Corporate Laws: How to ensure business decisions are within legal boundaries?
  • Other Laws: How to manage foreign exchange risks?
  • Income Tax: How to minimise and maximise tax outflow and maximize value?
  • Indirect Taxes: How to navigate India’s indirect tax system?
  • Art of Communication: How to pitch, persuade, & inspire like a leader?
  • Consolidate Financial Statements for a Multi-Subsidiary Entity
  • Compute Tax Liability for Individuals Across Multiple Income Heads
  • Prepare a GST Return and Analyse Input Tax Credit Adjustments

Master Compliance, Taxation, and Audit Basics

Gain practical expertise in taxation, auditing, and cost management while applying these concepts in real-world business contexts and mock audit exercises.

  • In Class : Core Courses
  • Hands-on Projects
  • Auditing & Ethics: How to perform an independent and credible audit?
  • Cost & Management Accounting: How to control your unit economics?
  • Financial Management: How to use finance to power better business decisions?
  • Strategic Management: How to grow business and tap into new markets?
  • Intro to Powerpoint & Excel: How to combine fact finding with compelling story telling?
  • Business Finance with Tally: How to manage the financial engine using Tally?
  • AI Tools in Trading: How to leverage AI and boost productivity?
  • Build a Cost Control and Budgeting Model for a Manufacturing Firm
  • Develop a Capital Budgeting Model Using NPV and IRR Analysis
  • Conduct a Risk-Based Audit Simulation and Draft an Audit Report
  • Record and Reconcile Full Business Transactions in Tally ERP

Advance into Strategic Finance and Assurance

Dive deeper into advanced accounting, strategic cost management, governance, and assurance. Learn to evaluate businesses, manage risk, and maintain compliance at scale.

  • In Class : Core Courses
  • Hands-on Projects
  • Financial Reporting: How to interpret efficiency and growth?
  • Advanced Financial Management: How to balance growth with financial discipline?
  • Advanced Auditing and Ethics: How to apply an ethical lens to financial figures?
  • Prepare Consolidated Financial Statements under Latest IND AS Standards
  • Design a Corporate Financing and Risk Management Strategy for a Firm
  • Conduct an Audit Simulation Focusing on Risk, Fraud, and Materiality Assessment

Emerge as a Complete Finance Professional

Apply everything through internships, valuation projects, and audit simulations, preparing to lead in accounting, taxation, or corporate advisory roles globally.

  • In Class : Core Courses
  • Hands-on Projects
  • Indirect Tax Laws: How to ensure efficiency in indirect taxation systems?
  • Integrated Business Solutions: How to tie strategy, finance, tax, and ethics together?
  • Tax Laws & International Taxation: How to manage corporate & cross-border taxation?
  • Prepare and Optimise Tax Filings Including Cross-Border Transactions
  • Conduct GST and Customs Compliance Analysis Through Real Case Studies

Build the Foundations of Finance and Economics

Master the fundamentals of accounting, micro and macro economics, statistics, and ethics, laying the groundwork to understand how businesses, markets, and financial systems function.

  • In Class : Core Courses
  • Hands-on Projects
  • Microeconomics : How to predict demand and supply?
  • Fundamentals of Accounting : How to read financials like an investor?
  • Macroeconomics : How a country’s economy work?
  • Business Statistics : How to decode the signal from the noise?
  • Introduction to Stock Markets : How to identify the winning stocks?
  • Excel for Finance : How to structure financial data for modelling?
  • Fixed Income : How to price bonds and understand investor confidence?
  • Quantitative Methods I : How to turn financial data into profit-making insights?
  • Corporate Finance I : How to optimise capital allocation and cost of capital?
  • Art of Communication : How to pitch, persuade, & inspire like a leader?
  • AI Tools in Trading : How to leverage AI and boost productivity?
  • Design a Winning Stock-Portfolio Using Technical Analysis
  • Perform Forensic Analysis of a Company’s Financial Statements
  • Build a Predictive Inventory Model for a Client in Manufacturing Industry

Master the Core of Investment and Valuation

Deepen your knowledge with corporate finance, fixed income, equities, and derivatives. Learn to analyze markets, build financial models, and make data-driven investment decisions.

  • In Class : Core Courses
  • Hands-on Projects
  • Corporate Finance II : How to manage a company like a CFO?
  • Quantitative Methods II : How to use futures and options to hedge risk?
  • Alternative Investments I : How to leverage REITs and commodities for diversification?
  • Financial Reporting and Analysis I : How to analyse ratios and footnotes?
  • Financial Reporting and Analysis II : How to interpret assets, efficiency, and growth?
  • Ethics & Professional Standards : How to judge integrity and governance mechanisms?
  • Portfolio Management : How to turn uncertainty into opportunity?
  • Sustainable Finance & ESG Investing : How to price risk & opportunity sustainably?
  • Private Equity & Venture Capital : How to fuel innovation through private capital?
  • Investment Banking : How to identify opportunities and close deals?
  • Industry Primers : How to understand ways of working across industries?
  • Prepare and Analyse Full Financial Statements for a Public Company
  • Use Hypothesis Testing to Identify Alpha in Equity Returns
  • Perform Ratio and Valuation Analysis Using Adjusted Financial Statements
  • Construct and Optimise a Diversified Portfolio Using CAPM Principles

Advance into Risk, Strategy, and Asset Management

Explore advanced derivatives, alternative investments, and risk management while tackling CFA-aligned projects. Learn to manage portfolios and assess investment strategies.

  • In Class : Core Courses
  • Hands-on Projects
  • Fixed Income II : How to navigate interest rate risks?
  • Quantitative Methods III : How to quantify market signals and build winning strategies?
  • Economics for Investment Management : How to drive strategic investing & risk control?
  • Investment Exit Strategies : How to plan, structure & close investment exits?
  • Equity Valuation : How to assess a company's value and optimize investments?
  • Advanced Financial Reporting & Analysis : How to use reports to assess performance?
  • Corporate Issuers : How to optimize corporate debt and equity issuances?
  • Portfolio Management & Wealth Planning: How to evaluate, adjust, & optimise portfolios?
  • Derivatives II : How to hedge against uncertainty without losing upside?
  • Ethics & Professional Standards II : How to review existing professional standards?
  • Alternative Investments II : How to multiply returns and manage volatility?
  • Develop a Time-Series Model to Forecast Asset Returns
  • Value an Options Portfolio Using the Black–Scholes Model
  • Design and Backtest an Active Multi-Factor Equity Strategy
  • Recommend an Optimal Capital Structure and Dividend Policy for a Public Firm

Transition from Analyst to Investment Professional

Apply your learning through internships and capstones, mastering portfolio construction, ethics, and compliance, preparing to operate as a finance professional in global markets.

  • In Class : Core Courses
  • Hands-on Projects
  • Advanced Portfolio Management Strategies : How to optimize for alpha and beta?
  • Portfolio Construction : How to balance growth and stability in your portfolio?
  • Portfolio Performance & Attribution : How to use attribution for investment decisions?
  • Derivatives & Risk Management : How to implement risk management strategies?
  • Ethics & Professional Standards III : How to integrate ethics into investment strategies?
  • Asset Allocation : How to strategically allocate assets for long-term success?
  • Construct and Optimise an Institutional Portfolio Based on Risk–Return Objectives
  • Design and Backtest a Hedging Strategy Using Options and Futures
  • Build and Rebalance a Multi-Asset Portfolio Incorporating Alternative Investments
  • Develop a Strategic Asset Allocation Framework Under Different Market Scenarios

Play a
New Finance Role Each Term

Every term, you step into a different chair on the finance floor, owning real deliverables, tools, and decisions.

Equity Research Challenge

Beat the Sensex

WealthPreneur (Build Your Own WM Firm)

Investment Banking Challenge

PE/VC Challenge

The Tax Playbook

The Financial Audit Challenge

Portfolio Management

Equity Research Challenge

Beat the Sensex

WealthPreneur (Build Your Own WM Firm)

Investment Banking Challenge

PE/VC Challenge

The Tax Playbook

The Financial Audit Challenge

Portfolio Management

Equity Analyst

What You Will Learn

Analyse a listed company’s financials, growth drivers, and competitive edge.

Build valuations using DCF and comparables to form an investment thesis.

Create an equity report & model using financial statements & Excel/Python.

Mutual Fund Manager

Frame a hypothesis and construct a ₹50L multi-asset virtual portfolio.

Analyse portfolio performance, separating skill from luck.

Deliver a strategy report with backtest data using time-series and risk tools.

Wealth Manager

Design HNI client portfolios with goal-based allocation and tax optimisation.

Test strategies through pilot planning and client feedback.

Present a full wealth plan and allocation model built using MF/ETF tools.

Investment Banker

Identify acquisition targets and analyse synergies and deal structures.

Create and defend valuation and transaction strategies before a mock board.

Submit an M&A deck and model built with comps, precedents, and merger tools.

Startup Investment Analyst

Develop a sector thesis mapping market trends, ICPs, and unit economics.

Identify 10–15 startups, interact with founders, and pitch your investment picks.

Deliver an investment memo and exit model using market and growth analysis.

Tax Strategist

Tackle cross-border taxation, DTAA, and transfer pricing cases.

Draft compliant, tax-efficient cash flow and repatriation strategies.

Submit a tax report and computation model using Income Tax & GST frameworks.

CFO Office: Controls & Assurance

Investigate financial discrepancies and identify audit risks.

Test internal controls and propose remediation strategies.

Present an audit report and risk matrix built with IND-AS standards and Tally.

Portfolio Manager

Build and manage a diversified portfolio aligned to client goals.

Track performance and optimise risk–return through rebalancing.

Present a final portfolio report using Bloomberg terminals and analytics.

Immerse Yourself in Global Finance

Experience global markets firsthand through our Global Immersion
Programmes across leading financial capitals, from London to Singapore.

Where You’ll Go

Europe

Visit institutions like the UN, Rabobank, and Mercedes-Benz to explore diplomacy, sustainability-linked finance, and global trade.

Singapore

Learn from leaders at Temasek, Agoda, and Singtel in Singapore on private equity, fintech innovation, and ASEAN investment flows.

Japan

Study innovation and R&D with Rakuten, Nissan, and Kyoto Research Park, and interact with founders driving Japan’s financial ecosystem.

Dubai

Engage with DIFC, Emirates NBD, Dubai Investment Fund, and ADGM to explore wealth management, finance, and global trade across the Middle East.

Real Insights From
Our Faculty

Learn about lucrative career pathway post education in Finance

Sarthak Ahuja, CA & Investment Banker

How mastering global finance can unlock opportunities in high-stakes investment banking and beyond.

Nilay Arun, Founder, Alaavo Ventures

Why the CFA qualification is a career game-changer and the essential skills needed to excel in global finance.

Rajat Kumar, Managing Partner, Nandan Capital

How a CA foundation opens doors to top roles in investment banking, venture investing, and private equity.

Illinois Institute of Technology, US

Discover how 3+1 Dual Degree Programme with Illinois Tech enables our
students to study abroad & earn a globally recognised bachelor’s.

Immersion

Dive into our modules where business management meets hands-on
experience, giving you real-world skills that truly stand out.

The State Plate

Payal Polyplast

Sita Ram Diwan Chand

Learn Directly from
Future Recruiters

  • Learn from CAs, CFAs, fund managers, and financial leaders at Deloitte, Goldman Sachs, JP Morgan, NSE, Zeodha Fund House & more.

  • Step into a real finance role of an Equity Analyst, Investment Banker, Portfolio Manager, VC Analyst, Tax Strategist, & more.

  • Get 1-1 mentorship and shadow Masters in your preferred domain.

Priority Admissions Track
For Students with Foreign University Admits

In response to the global uncertainty and challenges students face with international admissions, we’re introducing the Priority Admissions Track at Masters' Union. This exclusive opportunity allows students studying abroad and those with offers from top global universities to skip the entrance test and GD rounds and fast-track directly to the interview stage with just their admit letter and SAT scores.

Application Closed

Enrollment Checklist
Admissions Criteria

01.
Academics

01.
Academics

Class X & XII Marks (Expected/Final) from one of the recognised boards including CBSE, ISC, IB, IGCSE etc. Both students with and without Mathematics in Class XII are eligible for the programme.

02.
Aptitude Score

02.
Aptitude Score

Scores in Masters' Union Scholarship (Aptitude) Test/SAT/ACT.

03.
Extra Curricular Activities

03.
Extra Curricular Activities

Applications would be evaluated for extracurricular achievements and projects.

04.
Communication & Creativity

04.
Communication & Creativity

We will assess these qualities through application essays, in-person business simulation, and personal interviews.

Who is an Ideal Candidate?

This programme is built for people who think in numbers and get genuinely curious about why markets move, why businesses succeed, and where money really gets made (and lost). If you enjoy working with data, solving complex problems, and staying sharp under pressure, you’ll feel at home here. Watch the video to know more.

Watch Video Download Student Report

Application
Timeline & Process

Rounds Dates Online Scholarship Test Interviews Application Fees Status Commencement Action
Round 1 15th Oct ’25 -
5th Dec ‘25		 Nov/Dec ‘25 Dec/Jan ‘25 INR 2,400/- Active Aug '26 Apply Now
Round 2 Dec '24 - 28th Feb '25 Mar/Apr/May '25 Mar/Apr/May '25 INR 3,000/- Closed Aug '25 Apply Now
Round 3 15th Mar '25 - 14th May '25 Mar/Apr/May '25 Mar/Apr/May '25 INR 3,500/- Closed Aug '25 Apply Now
Round 4 18th May '25 - 16th Jul '25 Jun/Jul '25 Jun/Jul '25 INR 4,000/- Closed Aug '25 Apply Now

Blueprint to a
Standout Application

Your application is more than just a form, it’s your first pitch to the admissions team. In this student-led video, Yashovardhan, a senior at Masters’ Union, shares the ultimate blueprint to crafting a stellar application, from structuring essays to avoiding common pitfalls.

01.

Complete the
Application

01.

Complete the
Application

Requirements

Essential Documents

  • School Marksheets of Class X and XI

  • 12th grade passing certificate for students graduating in either year 2024, 2025 or 2026

  • CA Foundation Exam Attempt: If you have attempted the CA Foundation exam, please provide your exam attempt details and any relevant documents.

Video Essay

  • After submitting Part 1 of the application and payment of application fees, you must submit your video essay within 7 days.

  • Upload your video to Google Drive and ensure it’s set to "Anyone with the link can view."

  • Video Length: Please keep the video under 2 minutes.

  • Hobbies: Highlight unique qualities that demonstrate your passion for finance, accounting, or investment analysis to make an impression on the admissions committee

  • Reference Video: For tips on creating a compelling video essay, refer to this helpful guide here.

Priority Admissions Track:

  • If you're studying abroad or have an offer from a top global university, you can fast-track your admission through our Priority Admissions Track. Skip the entrance test and group discussion rounds, and go directly to the interview stage. Simply submit your admit letter and SAT scores as part of your application to qualify for this expedited process.

02.

Shortlisting &
Scholarship Aptitude Test

02.

Shortlisting & Scholarship Aptitude Test

Requirements

About the Online Scholarship Test

  • Upon shortlisting, students will receive an online scholarship test. The test will focus on core finance skills, including data analysis, numerical ability, and logical reasoning. It will assess your aptitude for accounting, financial markets, and quantitative finance.

  • A practice test will be available, and students will have 14 days to complete the scholarship test.

  • Based on your aptitude scores, you will be shortlisted for a Boardroom Simulation. Preparation materials for the test can be found here.

  • Optional: Students can submit their scores from IPMAT, SAT, or CUET. If submitted, the scholarship test is optional.

  • Watch a student-led breakdown of a test strategy that can help you secure Masters’ Union Scholarship here.

03.

Schedule Boardroom
Simulation & Interview

03.

Schedule Boardroom Simulation & Interview

Requirements

About Boardroom Simulation & Interview

  • The in-person Boardroom Simulation will require candidates to analyze a company’s financials, assess key metrics, and make buy, hold, or sell recommendations in a team of 5-6 applicants.

  • The interview will be a 1:1 conversation with faculty or senior leadership, focusing on your financial analysis skills and your approach to investment decisions.

  • Both the Boardroom Simulation and interview will take place on the same day, across major cities in India, with Zoom options available in exceptional cases.

04.

Admission
Decision

04.

Admission Decision

Requirements

Status of Evaluation

Once shortlisted applicants have submitted their application along with their aptitude scores, the admissions team will respond with their final decision and the students will fall into three categories:

Accepted

  • Applicants who have been offered admission to Masters’ Union will receive their decision along with the financial structure for the upcoming programme.

  • They will have 7-21 days to confirm their admission through payment of the admission fee.

Rejected

  • Applicants rejected by the admissions team can apply to the programme again in the next academic year.

Waitlisted

  • Masters’ Union doesn’t maintain any ranked waitlist. Applicants placed in this category will have their admission dependent on the spots that might open up in the cohort.

  • The admission committee reconvenes to decide the results and promotion of candidates from the waitlisted to the accepted pool, regularly.

Lateral Entry: Transfer to Masters’ Union

Masters' Union offers a Lateral Entry pathway, allowing students in their first year at any other university to transfer seamlessly into our cutting-edge Undergraduate/IPM Programme in Technology & Business Management. Take the leap and elevate your academic experience with industry-aligned curriculum, expert mentorship, and real-world exposure. The online admission process is the same for all applicants.

Apply Now
Apply Now

Years Non-Refundable
Admission Fee
 4 Years
Tuition Fee
 3 years in India + 1 year in US
Global Track

Year 4 - INR 7,90,000/- INR 70,20,000/-*

Accommodation Type All Inclusive
Amount

Single Sharing (Per Month) INR 48,500/-

  • 01. Higher Studies
  • 02. Job Prospects

Graduate with an Integrated MBA in Five Years

UG students can choose a 5th year extension in Year 4 to earn an MBA, strengthening their careers in Investment Banking, Consulting, & Global Finance.

Study for a Year at Illinois Tech

Students have an option to spend the 4th Year at Illinois Tech Campus in Chicago and get a B.S. in Business Administration in the US.
Check Global Track

Prepare for Global Postgraduate Programmes

Get expert support for MS Finance, MFin, MBA, and PhD applications, including GMAT/GRE prep and mentorship from alumni at top global universities.

Over 100+ Marquee Recruiters

Top recruiters include AmEx, Bloomberg, Citi Bank, KPMG, PwC, EY, Aditya Birla Capital, Kotak, and more.

Unlock High-Impact Roles After CA

Step into careers across audit, taxation, consulting, and corporate finance, driving business growth and financial strategy for top enterprises.

Command Global Finance Careers After CFA

Open doors to roles in investment banking, equity research, portfolio management, & wealth advisory across established finance centers worldwide.

Download Placement Report

Potential  Job Roles

Our programme equips students with the financial, analytical, and regulatory acumen to thrive in high-impact roles across global finance. From driving M&A deals to advising HNIs and decoding complex audits, graduates are prepared to lead in investment banking, asset management, risk advisory, and beyond.

Investment Banking Analyst

Equity Research Associate/Analyst

Credit Analyst

Investment Analyst (Buy-Side)

Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A) Analyst

Valuation Analyst/Associate

Transaction Services Associate

Internal Audit / Risk Analyst

Audit Associate/Trainee

Tax Analyst (Direct or Indirect Tax)

Internal Audit Associate

Financial Controller Trainee

Compliance Officer Assistant

Forensic Audit Analyst

Parent’s Perspective
The Masters’ Union Transformation

Witnessing their child evolve into a confident, ambitious, and industry-ready professional is every parent’s dream. At Masters’ Union, our hands-on, immersive approach ensures that students not only excel academically but also develop real-world skills, leadership qualities, and an entrepreneurial mindset. Hear from parents who have seen this transformation first-hand.

Student Internship Experience

Sneha Panthary

Treasury, Paytm

Class of 2027

“During my Treasury internship at Paytm, I worked on compliance, audits, and mutual fund management. I gained deep insights into regulatory frameworks, supported internal audits, and strengthened my understanding of risk management and corporate treasury operations.”

1/10

Jay Khemka

Investment Analysis, Nandan Growth Fund

Class of 2027

“As an Investment Analyst, I evaluated investment opportunities alongside bankers, analysing financials, assessing risks, and identifying gaps. I also participated in CEO meetings during deal negotiations, gaining exposure to strategic thinking and high-stakes financial decision-making.”

2/10

Ritvika Khanna

GST Filings & Audit, KPMG

Class of 2027

“During my internship at KPMG, I identified a ₹30 Cr tax benefit in my first week, a pivotal moment that greatly accelerated my growth. With a supportive manager who challenged me, I got opportunities to represent KPMG at conferences, expanding my expertise, network, and confidence.”

3/10

Shivangi Duttaupadhyay

Spectrum Banking, Yes Bank

Class of 2027

“At Yes Bank, I worked on wealth management and equity advisory, analysing equity options, tracking stock performance, and recommending investments. I engaged with 15–20 HNIs, gaining insights into client profiling, risk appetite, and personalised financial planning.”

4/10

Pulastya Jain

Portfolio Analysis, SEP

Class of 2027

“At Sweat Equity Partners, I led market research and team interviews for a portfolio company, built a detailed financial model, and managed interns to create a grant pitch deck. This experience sharpened my analytical thinking, leadership, and communication skills.”

5/10

Palak Dua

Risk & Audit, EY

Class of 2027

“Working with the Risk and Audit team at a Big 4 firm as a first-year student gave me hands-on exposure to financial planning, quantitative analysis, and compliance. I built strong skills in financial modelling and gained a solid grasp of India’s regulatory framework.”

6/10

Devansh Asawa

Business Finance, Shadowfax

Class of 2027

“During my internship at Shadowfax, I handled financial reconciliations and dispute resolution for clients like Tata Cliq and Hopscotch. I also worked on cost optimisation by analysing expenses and negotiating vendor rates, which directly improved operational efficiency and boosted profit margins.”

7/10

Ananya Singhania

Strategic Planning & Market Analysis, PwC

Class of 2027

“At PwC, I was entrusted with ownership from day one, enhancing internal financial analysis tools and collaborating with cross-functional teams. This experience provided me with valuable insights into global financial systems and market dynamics.”

8/10

Dhruv Lakra

Venture Capital, Sauce.VC

Class of 2027

“At Sauce VC, I sourced 15+ early-stage consumer startups, reviewed 20+ pitch decks, and conducted due diligence via founder calls, surveys, and market research. I also built category trackers to spot trends and learned how investors evaluate founders, scalability, and differentiation.”

9/10

Gunit Royzada

Investment Research, Analysis & Reporting, Waterfield

Class of 2027

“At Waterfield Advisors, India's largest multi-family office, I worked in the Global Investment Advisory team, analysing offshore mutual funds, reviewing UHNI portfolios, and automating workflows using Excel, Bloomberg, and Morningstar. This gave me direct exposure to global investment strategies and portfolio management.”

10/10

Frequently Asked
Questions

  • General
  • Video Essay
  • Scholarship (Aptitude) Test
  • CA/CFA Pathway
  • Boardroom Simulation/ PI
  • Fees
  • Financing Options

Can students submit their documents/ certificates later if they need time to get it from school?

Students can fill the form with certificates that are available. Later on, they can simply provide the remaining documents to the team as the application processes.

Is it mandatory for students to submit their class XI marksheet?

Yes, it is. However, if they don’t have the Class XI marksheet (PCM), they can upload an application explaining the reason for its unavailability at the time of filling out the application and attach the PDF in place of the marksheet.

If a student has lost their X result (or any document) which is mandatory to upload, how can students fill the form?

They can upload an application stating the reason for not having it under the 10th marksheet upload section. Ensure to send an official email at admission@mastersunion.org later, once the students obtain it.

Can students edit the video essay?

Yes, they can but it is not needed. We are judging students on their communication and confidence and not their video editing skills.

Is there a sample video essay?

Yes, here is a sample video.

When will students get their application results and get shortlisted for the further round?

Results can be expected to be announced anywhere between 15-30 days after the commencement of the deadline. Post receiving information on the shortlist, students will receive an online Scholarship (Aptitude) Test that they have to take online from home.

What if a student has submitted the video but didn't get the Scholarship (Aptitude) Test mail yet?

The email will be sent once the video is evaluated and students are selected. Results can be expected to be announced anywhere between 15-30 days after the submission of the video. Find more details here.

What are the passing marks?

We look into the profile holistically before selecting or rejecting a student. However, please note that scoring 35 marks (out of a total of 45) is the eligibility criteria for scholarship opportunities.

Can students submit their SAT Score instead of taking the Masters’ Union Scholarship (Aptitude) Test?

Yes, students also have the option to provide their JEE/IPMAT/SAT/CUET scores. The Scholarship (Aptitude) Test becomes optional for those who have provided their scores and they will be shortlisted on the basis of the score. Please note that the scholarship test is not just for students looking for scholarships but is also an essential step of our admission process which enables us to assess the analytical capabilities of the student.

How much time do students get to prepare for the Scholarship (Aptitude) Test?

Students will get a 7-day period to finish the Scholarship (Aptitude) Test. Find more details here.

Can students use calculators?

Yes, students are allowed to use calculators. We suggest they keep a pen and paper handy with them during the test and a glass of water.

Is there any negative marking?

There is no negative marking.

What is considered a good JEE/IPMAT/CUET percentile?

Percentile greater than or equal to 90 is considered good.

What is a good SAT score?

Students who choose to submit their SAT scores, a score greater than or equal to 1500 is considered good. Please note the test score is one aspect of the application. Student profiles are evaluated holistically.

Can students get an extension to appear for the Scholarship (Aptitude) test?

They can write an email to the ugadmissions@mastersunion.org , attaching the specific documents explaining why an extension is needed. Extension can be granted in genuine cases, as per consideration via email.

When will the results for the Scholarship (Aptitude) Test be announced?

Results can be expected to be announced anywhere between 15-30 days after the test deadline.

What are the "CA/CFA Pathway" mentioned in the title

The curriculum is structured to align with the core content requirements for professional certifications like Chartered Accountant (CA) and Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA). This allows students to simultaneously prepare for these certifications alongside their degree, giving them a significant head start on their professional qualification journey.

Is it mandatory to choose a certification (CA or CFA) or sit for the professional exams?

No, the decision to sit for the external professional exams (CA or CFA) is entirely optional. The programme is designed to provide the curriculum and preparation necessary for these pathways. However, the choice to pursue the official certification, including registering for and taking the external exams, remains with the student. Your Bachelor's degree from Masters' Union is a stand-alone qualification.

When do I need to choose between the CA or the CFA pathway?

The decision should be made before the beginning of the course. We offer dedicated pre-enrollment guidance to help you make an informed choice.

Is switching from CA to CFA allowed?

    Yes, in principle, it is allowed, but the process has two parts:

  • Internal Academic Flexibility (Masters' Union): If a student decides in the first year to switch their focus, then you will not lose the credits from your academic degree. However, we allow case by case basis in the first semester only.

  • External Certification (ICAI/CFA Institute): This is the main point of clarity: you cannot switch credit between the two certification bodies. CA (ICAI) and CFA (CFA Institute) are independent, external qualifications.
    If you pay the registration fee and clear a level of one (e.g., CA Foundation) and then switch to the other (CFA), you will have to start from Level I of the new certification. The fees paid for the first certification are non-transferable and non-refundable to the new certification.

Is switching from CFA to CA allowed?

No, this is not allowed given CA has a lot of statutory components and a mandatory articleship.

If a student fails a level of the CA or CFA exam, does it affect their degree?

No, The UG Programme in Finance & Economics is an independent academic degree. Your Bachelor's degree is awarded solely based on your successful completion of the required academic coursework, internal exams, and industry projects mandated by Masters' Union.
Failure in any level of the professional examinations (CA or CFA) does not affect the student's academic standing or their ability to graduate with the Bachelor's degree from Masters' Union.

Are there regular practice tests and mentorship support to track progress?

Yes, regular practice tests, detailed assessments, and mock exams are an essential part of the programme to ensure the student is exam-ready. We provide extensive mentorship support to partner on the journey and track progress.

What kind of careers can I pursue after completing this programme?

Graduates will be prepared for roles across the financial spectrum, including but not limited to:

  • Investment Banking & Equity Research

  • Financial Consulting & Risk Management

  • Wealth Management & Portfolio Management

  • Corporate Finance & Treasury

  • Economic Analysis & Policy

Will I graduate with the UG Programme in Finance & Economics and also become a fully qualified CA/CFA Charter Holder at the exact same time (i.e., after 4 years)?

No, While the programme is 4 years, the final professional qualification for both CA and CFA will be achieved after you receive your degree. However, we will prepare you for your final level exams for both the qualifications.

Is there an option to switch to another Masters' Union UG programme (e.g., Tech & Business Management)?

Switching programs is subject to availability, eligibility criteria, and a formal internal transfer process. It is generally possible within the first year, provided you meet the academic criteria of the destination programme, though this is assessed on a case-by-case basis.

Do we have students doing any of these careers already from TBM?

Yes, students from the UG TBM are already actively pursuing and securing careers in the targeted finance roles, primarily through early and consistent internships and live projects.
Internships: UG TBM students have secured internships at major firms like EY, PwC, KPMG. They have also interned with VC firms like sauce.vc, waterfield advisors, Nandan Capital.

Do we register the students for professional certification through MU- is the exam and registration fee covered?

No, the professional certification fee needs to be paid separately.

Is Mathematics in Class 12 mandatory for admission to the Finance & Economics UG course?

No, mathematics in class 12 is not a mandatory eligibility requirement.

What is the proportion of Finance vs Economics in the course?

The UG Programme in Finance & Economics with CA/CFA Pathways is primarily weighted towards Finance (Applied), with Economics (Foundational) serving as a critical supporting pillar. The programme uses a strong foundation in Economics to provide context for a deep dive into Applied Finance, all while being infused with Data and Technology skills necessary for the modern financial professional. Since the curriculum is aligned to CA/CFA qualifications, The percentage of Economics in the curriculum is the same as its weightage in the CA/CFA professional syllabus.

Is the business simulation/ personal interview conducted online or offline?

The in-person boardroom simulation and interview will be conducted on the same day for a student. These will be scheduled across major cities (Delhi, Bengaluru, Mumbai, Ahmedabad, Indore, Hyderabad, Chandigarh) in India and over Zoom in exceptional cases.

What is a boardroom simulation? How to prepare for it?

The in-person boardroom simulation will require students to role-play as a CXO of a company and solve its business challenges in a team of 5-6 applicants. We will send them a case study to prepare for the boardroom simulation round 3-4 days prior for preparation. In this round, they will be judged on their creativity, communication, and teamwork skills. They will be participating in a group discussion for 45 minutes followed by a presentation (without any slides).

What happens in the personal interview, what kind of questions will be asked?

The interview will be a one-on-one conversation with our faculty or senior leadership. The questions will be around their application and motivations for applying to Masters' Union. The goal is to know more about students and what they want to achieve at Masters' Union.

Is there any detailed information about boardroom simulation/ personal interview?

  • The overall process will take around 3 hours.

  • The boardroom simulation will last 45 minutes followed by a 15 minute group presentation. Students will be working in teams of 4-6 students on a simulated business scenario, where students will be expected to make decisions, analyse data and present their findings.

  • The interview will be conducted by a senior leader or a faculty member at Masters’ Union and will be approximately 30 minutes long. They will be asked questions about their application including their achievements, essays and the video essay submitted.

  • Dress code for the event is smart casuals.

When will the results be declared?

CThe results are announced 30-45 days after the date of the interview. You can find more details here.

Is there a firm deadline for payment of the admission fee?

We provide students ample time till July of the academic year so that they can make their decision based on all the admission offers that they have. In case they still want an extension, the same can be granted based on an email request.

What is the mode of payment for the fees?

Mode of payment can be online, net banking, bank transfer or in some rare cases they can even send a cheque. Except for cash, we accept all payment methods.

What is the fee payment schedule?

Students can pay the fees semester wise. Starting every semester, they will be notified about the fees due by ugadmissions@mastersunion.org

Does Masters’ Union offer scholarships?

At Masters’ Union we believe that financial constraints should not pose any hindrance to students’ academic aspirations. We have merit and need-based scholarships for all deserving candidates. Students scoring above 35 out of 45 marks in the Masters’ Union Scholarship Aptitude test get a scholarship which can range from 10% - 25% of the tuition fee depending on the student’s performance in the Scholarship (Aptitude)Test and the overall profile of the applicant. For candidates keen on scholarship, we encourage students to submit their application early to allow for our admission counsellors to thoroughly review their scholarship case before presenting it to the admissions committee at the time of evaluation.

Is the scholarship for all four years or just for the first year?

Scholarship is awarded for all four years. If a student receives a 20% scholarship, they will receive a 20% scholarship every semester on their respective fees. However, in cases of misconduct or rule violations, disciplinary action may be taken.

Are there any financing options?

Yes, there are financial institutions that can provide loans for up to 95% of the programme fees to the admitted students. Read here for further details.
